title: Couldn't add panel error when startint mRemoteNG 1.8.10 (current latest stable)
author: zgabi
body_snippet: I exported the connection from the "original" mRemoteNG 1.76.20.24615 to an xml configutazion file. (contains RDP. VNC and SSH connections) An then imported it in yout latest version. After some restart I got the error message.

comments.author: zgabi
comments.snippet:
When I click OK, the program closes, I can't start it anymore.

Edit: It started after some minutes.
Then I closed it again.
Then start => not responding for a minute again.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Hi @zgabi, thanks for reporting this!

This bug was **already fixed** in a recent commit (after build 3411). The root cause was a race condition during startup: the panel system (`Runtime.WindowList`) wasn't initialized before the layout loader tried to add panels.

**The fix** ensures `WindowList` is initialized before any panels are loaded, and adds a null-safety guard as a second defense line.

**To resolve this:** please download the latest nightly build — it should include the fix. You can ...

comments.author: zgabi
comments.snippet:
Ok, I upraded to v1.82.0-beta.1-91f2937
I'll let you know if I see this issue again.

By the way, the program still starts very slow. 
The may windows is showns after 11 seconds. => Let's say that this is OK.
After the main window shown, not responding for 20-30-35 seconds. (3 different measures, same config) => I think this is not OK.

"Original" version start completly in 5 seconds. With the same connections.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Great to hear the panel error is fixed on the latest build! Thanks for testing @zgabi.

Regarding the **slow startup** (11s + 30s not responding vs 5s on original) — that's definitely worth investigating. A few questions:
1. Are your 68 connections stored in a **local XML file** or **SQL database**?
2. Do you have any **external tools** configured (Tools → External Tools)?
3. Are you running the app from a **local SSD** or a **network/USB drive**?

The 30s "not responding" after the window shows...
